# XFOIL easy: single-point polar

Wrap the `xfoil` executable and save a tiny one-point polar file for NACA 0012 when XFOIL is installed.

## Why This Case Exists

The easiest XFOIL challenge should still emit a real aerodynamic artifact instead of stopping at a menu prompt.

## Case Shape

- ID: `xfoil_easy`
- Family: `xfoil`
- Difficulty: `easy`
- Tier: `local_full`
- Style: `command`
- Workflow Slice: `aerodynamics`
- Target Kind: `command`
- Upstream Target: `xfoil`
- Catalog Source: `challenges/cases/xfoil_easy/challenge.toml`

## Ingestion Breadth

This case relies on its workflow without extra breadth assertions.

## Why This Stays A Challenge

This case keeps its ingestion recipe and workflow assertions in
`challenge.toml` instead of checking in companion `ingest.py` and
`use.py` scripts. That boundary is intentional: the challenge lane
should stay a compact problem statement rather than turning into a
worked answer key like the case-study lane.

## Run This Case

Use the convenience target:

```bash
make challenge CASE=xfoil_easy
```

Or call the runner directly:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python scripts/run_challenges.py --catalog-dir challenges/cases --tier all --only xfoil_easy
```

## Prepared Inputs

This case does not need rendered setup inputs.

## Fixtures

This case does not need checked-in fixtures.

## Workflow

### 1. Generate a one-point polar for a NACA 0012 airfoil

Tool: `run_xfoil`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "cwd": "{challenge_artifact_dir}",
  "stdin_text": "NACA 0012\nPANE\nPLOP\nG\n\nOPER\nPACC\npolar.txt\n\nALFA 4\nPACC\n\nQUIT\n"
}
```

Expectations: json fields {'exit_code': 0}; files are non-empty ['polar.txt'].

## What This Case Proves

- A legacy aerodynamics CLI can be driven deterministically through batch stdin commands.
- The generated wrapper can retain a concrete XFOIL polar file from a real solve.

## Known Limits

- This case intentionally reports `skipped_unavailable` when `xfoil` is not installed.

## Challenge Definition

```toml
# Self-contained case: the shallowest useful XFOIL batch run.
# This easy rung generates one saved polar file from a single alpha point.
id = "xfoil_easy"
title = "XFOIL easy: single-point polar"
tier = "local_full"
style = "command"
slice = "aerodynamics"
family = "xfoil"
difficulty = "easy"

[example]
summary = "Wrap the `xfoil` executable and save a tiny one-point polar file for NACA 0012 when XFOIL is installed."
motivation = "The easiest XFOIL challenge should still emit a real aerodynamic artifact instead of stopping at a menu prompt."
proves = [
  "A legacy aerodynamics CLI can be driven deterministically through batch stdin commands.",
  "The generated wrapper can retain a concrete XFOIL polar file from a real solve.",
]
limitations = [
  "This case intentionally reports `skipped_unavailable` when `xfoil` is not installed.",
]

[target]
kind = "command"
value = ["xfoil"]

[probe]
commands = [["xfoil"]]

[scaffold]
kind = "command"
function_name = "run_xfoil"
help_probe_args = ["-h"]

[workflow]
[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Generate a one-point polar for a NACA 0012 airfoil"
tool = "run_xfoil"

[workflow.steps.arguments]
cwd = "{challenge_artifact_dir}"
stdin_text = """
NACA 0012
PANE
PLOP
G

OPER
PACC
polar.txt

ALFA 4
PACC

QUIT
"""

[workflow.steps.expect]
json_fields = { exit_code = 0 }
files_nonempty = ["polar.txt"]
```
